name: Word Wizard
description: Word play, anagrams, letter puzzles, and language tricks to test your vocab!
author: Game Night Crew

questions.question: Which of these words is spelled correctly?
questions.explanation: D-E-F-I-N-I-T-E-L-Y. One of the most commonly misspelled words in English. If you got this, you're already ahead!

questions.question: What is the plural of 'sheep'?
questions.explanation: Sheep is one of those weird English words where the plural is the same as the singular. One sheep, many sheep!

questions.question: Which word means the opposite of 'ancient'?
questions.explanation: Modern is the opposite of ancient. The others are all synonyms or related to being old. Easy one to warm up!

questions.question: What does the 'SA' in 'RSA' stand for?
questions.explanation: Republic of South Africa! If you're playing this game and didn't know that, eish...

questions.question: How many letters are in the word 'ALPHABET'?
questions.explanation: A-L-P-H-A-B-E-T = 8 letters. Don't confuse it with how many letters are in THE alphabet (26)!

questions.question: Which of these is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards)?
questions.explanation: LEVEL reads L-E-V-E-L both ways. A lekker clean palindrome!

questions.question: What word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it?
questions.explanation: The word 'short' becomes 'shorter' when you add 'er'. Literally shorter! Love a good word trick.

questions.question: How many vowels (A, E, I, O, U) are in the word 'EDUCATION'?
questions.explanation: E-D-U-C-A-T-I-O-N: the vowels are E, U, A, I, O = 5 vowels. Education has all 5 vowels, actually!

questions.question: In South African slang, what does 'lekker' mean?
questions.explanation: Lekker means nice, great, or enjoyable. It's probably the most essential SA slang word there is!

questions.question: Which letter of the alphabet comes after 'P' and before 'R'?
questions.explanation: Q sits between P and R. O-P-Q-R-S. Simple alphabet knowledge!

questions.question: Unscramble: ROITPARE — it's a South African city.
questions.explanation: ROITPARE unscrambles to PRETORIA! P-R-E-T-O-R-I-A rearranged. Our jacaranda-lined capital city!

questions.question: How many official languages does South Africa have?
questions.explanation: South Africa has 12 official languages: Zulu, Xhosa, Afrikaans, English, Sepedi, Tswana, Sotho, Tsonga, Swati, Venda, Ndebele, and South African Sign Language (added in 2023)!

questions.question: Which word contains all 5 vowels (A, E, I, O, U) exactly once?
questions.explanation: EDUCATION: E-D-U-C-A-T-I-O-N contains A, E, I, O, U each exactly once. Sequoia has all 5 too but also repeats none — however Education is the clearest answer. Beautiful repeats letters.

questions.question: What common English word has 3 letters, all of them the same?
questions.explanation: There is no common English word with 3 identical letters! AAA is an abbreviation, not a word. ZZZ represents sleeping but isn't a real word. Don't overthink it!

questions.question: What is the longest word you can type using only the top row of a keyboard (QWERTYUIOP)?
questions.explanation: TYPEWRITER uses only letters from Q-W-E-R-T-Y-U-I-O-P. It's 10 letters and one of the longest top-row-only words. Pretty ironic, ne?

questions.question: How many 3-letter words can you make from the letters in 'CAT' (using each letter only once, any order)?
questions.explanation: CAT and ACT. There are 6 permutations (CAT, CTA, ACT, ATC, TCA, TAC) but only CAT and ACT are real English words.

questions.question: What SA slang word completes this: 'That braai was ___!' (meaning it was amazing)?
questions.explanation: Befok means amazing or crazy (in a good way)! Kak means rubbish, dwaal means dazed, and dof means stupid. Know your slang!

questions.question: Which of these words is an anagram of 'LISTEN'?
questions.explanation: Both SILENT and TINSEL use the exact same letters as LISTEN: L-I-S-T-E-N. Two anagrams for the price of one!

questions.question: In the word 'MISSISSIPPI', how many times does the letter 'S' appear?
questions.explanation: M-I-S-S-I-S-S-I-P-P-I: S appears at positions 3, 4, 6, 7 = 4 times. Those double S's are sneaky!

questions.question: Put these SA slang words in order from most positive to most negative meaning:
questions.explanation: Lekker (great) > Kif (cool) > Eina (ouch/painful) > Kak (terrible). From good vibes to bad vibes!

questions.question: What common English word has 3 consecutive double letters?
questions.explanation: BOOKKEEPER: OO-KK-EE — three consecutive pairs of double letters! It's the most famous example of this pattern in English.

questions.question: How many letters are in the longest official South African language name 'SISWATI'? (Count carefully!)
questions.explanation: S-I-S-W-A-T-I = 7 letters. Not too tricky, but you need to count carefully and not second-guess yourself!

questions.question: What word in the English language is always spelled incorrectly?
questions.explanation: The word 'incorrectly' is always spelled I-N-C-O-R-R-E-C-T-L-Y — which IS 'incorrectly'! A classic wordplay trick.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a real Afrikaans word that's entered English?
questions.explanation: Flamboyant comes from French, not Afrikaans! Apartheid (separateness), aardvark (earth pig), and springbok (jumping buck) are all Afrikaans words used in English.

questions.question: How many different letters appear in the word 'ABRACADABRA'?
questions.explanation: A-B-R-A-C-A-D-A-B-R-A has these unique letters: A, B, R, C, D = 5 different letters. A appears 5 times, B twice, R twice, C once, D once.

questions.question: What is special about the sentence: 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog'?
options: It uses every letter of the alphabet
Every word has a different number of letters
questions.explanation: It's a pangram — a sentence that contains every letter of the alphabet at least once! It's been used to test typewriters and keyboards for over a century.

questions.question: Put these English words in order from FEWEST to MOST silent letters:
options: Queue (4 silent: U, E, U, E)
questions.explanation: Knife = 1 (K). Knight = 2 (K, GH). Daughter = 3 (A, U, GH are silent). Queue = 4 (UEUE). English spelling is absolutely mad!

questions.question: Which word can mean both a container AND to engage in a boxing match?
questions.explanation: BOX: a container (cardboard box) AND to fight with fists (boxing). English loves its double meanings! Ring and case also have dual meanings, but BOX fits both definitions most cleanly.

questions.question: Which word can be placed before 'LIGHT', 'BREAK', and 'DREAM' to form three new words?
questions.explanation: DAYLIGHT, DAYBREAK, DAYDREAM — all real compound words! Sunlight works but sunbreak and sundream don't.

questions.question: Put these famously long words in order from FEWEST to MOST letters:
questions.explanation: Antidisestablishmentarianism (28) < Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious (34) < Hippopotomonstrosesquippedalianism (36) < Pneumonoultramicroscopicsilicovolcanoconiosis (45). From big to absolutely massive!

questions.question: How many letters are in 'PNEUMONOULTRAMICROSCOPICSILICOVOLCANOCONIOSIS' — the longest word in major English dictionaries?
questions.explanation: P-N-E-U-M-O-N-O-U-L-T-R-A-M-I-C-R-O-S-C-O-P-I-C-S-I-L-I-C-O-V-O-L-C-A-N-O-C-O-N-I-O-S-I-S = 45 letters. It's a lung disease caused by inhaling very fine silica dust. Good luck spelling it!

questions.question: Which 6-letter word contains the letters A, B, C, D, E, and F (not necessarily all different)?
questions.explanation: DEFACE: D-E-F-A-C-E contains D, E, F, A, C, E — that's all 6 letters A through F represented! (E appears twice, but every letter from A-F is present.)

questions.question: Which word becomes its own opposite when you put 'IM' in front of it?
questions.explanation: All of them! Possible/Impossible, Proper/Improper, Patient/Impatient — all become their own opposites with the IM prefix. English is weird like that.

questions.question: In Scrabble, the letter 'Z' is worth 10 points. The word 'QUIZ' (without any multipliers) scores how many points? (Q=10, U=1, I=1, Z=10)
questions.explanation: Q(10) + U(1) + I(1) + Z(10) = 22 points. QUIZ is a powerhouse Scrabble word!

questions.question: The Afrikaans word 'braai' comes from the Dutch word 'braden'. What does 'braden' literally mean?
questions.explanation: Braden means 'to roast' in Dutch. So braai literally means to roast — which is exactly what we do to our boerewors and chops!

questions.question: How many words in English end in '-GRY'? (Think carefully — there are fewer than you think!)
questions.explanation: Only 2 common English words end in -GRY: ANGRY and HUNGRY. This is a famous trick question — people often think there must be a third, but there really isn't one in common usage!

questions.question: What English word has the most consecutive consonants?
questions.explanation: STRENGTHS has 8 consonants and only 1 vowel. The string NGTHS gives 5 consecutive consonants. TWELFTHS also has a strong consonant cluster but STRENGTHS is the classic answer for most consonants relative to vowels.

questions.question: In isiZulu, the click consonant 'c' sounds similar to:
options: A cluck like calling a horse
questions.explanation: The isiZulu 'c' click is a dental click, similar to the 'tsk tsk' sound of disapproval you make by pulling your tongue off the back of your teeth. Now try saying 'Gcwala'!

questions.question: The longest one-syllable word in English is often cited as 'STRENGTHS'. How many letters does it have?
questions.explanation: S-T-R-E-N-G-T-H-S = 9 letters, all in one syllable! Some argue 'SCHMALTZED' (10 letters) but that's debatable. STRENGTHS is the standard answer.

questions.question: Which of these words is spelled the same in Afrikaans and English?
questions.explanation: TAXI is spelled the same in both languages! Restaurant is 'restaurant' in both too actually, but taxi is the most universally identical across many languages.

questions.question: The word 'THEREIN' contains how many words within it (without rearranging letters)? Count words of 2+ letters.
questions.explanation: THE, THERE, THEREIN, HE, HER, HERE, HEREIN, ERE, REIN, IN = 10 words! Some also count RE and EI as abbreviations, but standard answer is 10 common English words.

questions.question: What is the only English word that ends in '-MT'?
questions.explanation: DREAMT is the only common English word ending in -MT. It's the past tense of 'dream' (British English). Americans tend to use 'dreamed' instead.

questions.question: Which of these letter combinations appears most frequently at the start of English words?
questions.explanation: TH is the most common starting digraph in English — think THE, THAT, THIS, THERE, THEY, THEIR, THEM, THAN, THROUGH... basically all the most common words!

questions.question: The letter 'E' is the most common letter in English. In a standard game of Scrabble, how many 'E' tiles are there?
questions.explanation: There are 12 E tiles in a standard Scrabble set (English version). That's more than any other letter! Second is A and I with 9 each.

questions.question: In the NATO phonetic alphabet, what word represents the letter 'J'?
questions.explanation: J = Juliet in the NATO alphabet (Alpha, Bravo, Charlie... Golf, Hotel, India, JULIET). Used worldwide by pilots and military.

questions.question: How many valid English words can be made by rearranging ALL 4 letters of 'STOP'? (Include STOP itself)
questions.explanation: STOP, TOPS, SPOT, POTS, OPTS, and POST = 6 words! Most people miss at least one. S-T-O-P is one of the most anagram-rich 4-letter combos in English.

questions.question: What is the shortest word in English that contains the letters A, B, C, D, E, and F?
questions.explanation: FEEDBACK (8 letters): F-E-E-D-B-A-C-K contains A, B, C, D, E, and F. BOLDFACE (8 letters) also works: B-O-L-D-F-A-C-E. Both are 8 letters but FEEDBACK is the more well-known answer.

questions.question: In the word 'STRENGTHS' (9 letters, 1 syllable), how many phonemes (distinct sounds) does it have?
questions.explanation: S-T-R-E-NG-TH-S = 7 phonemes: /s/, /t/, /r/, /ɛ/, /ŋ/, /θ/, /s/. The 'ng' is one sound and 'th' is one sound. Phonemes aren't the same as letters!

questions.question: What is the only word in the English language where all five vowels appear in reverse alphabetical order (U, O, I, E, A)?
questions.explanation: UNCOMPLIMENTARY: U-N-C-O-M-P-L-I-M-E-N-T-A-R-Y. The vowels in order of appearance: U, O, I, E, A — reverse alphabetical! A remarkable word.

questions.question: Put these English letters in order from MOST to LEAST frequently used:
questions.explanation: The most common English letters in order: E (13%), T (9.1%), A (8.2%), O (7.5%). ETAO is the beginning of the famous letter frequency ranking! Cryptographers know this by heart.

questions.question: How many words in the English language can be made from the periodic table symbols? For example, 'BaCoN' (Barium-Cobalt-Nitrogen). Approximately how many common English words can be spelled this way? (Nearest hundred)
questions.explanation: Approximately 600 common English words can be spelled using periodic table element symbols! Examples: BaCoN, CuTe, SiLvEr, CArBON, IRoN, TIN, etc. The exact count varies by dictionary, but ~600 is the accepted estimate.

questions.question: The word 'SET' holds a Guinness World Record. What is it?
options: Most definitions of any English word
questions.explanation: SET has the most definitions of any word in the English language — over 430 different meanings in the Oxford English Dictionary! From 'set a table' to 'a set of cards' to 'the sun sets' to 'set in stone'. Mental!

questions.question: The isiZulu word 'isithombe' means 'picture/photo'. How many of South Africa's 11 spoken official languages use click consonants?
questions.explanation: Four SA languages use click consonants: isiZulu, isiXhosa, isiNdebele, and Siswati (to a lesser extent). Sesotho and Setswana have some borrowed clicks but they're not core to the language.

questions.question: What is the longest English word that can be typed using only the left hand on a standard QWERTY keyboard (QWERTASDFGZXCVB)?
questions.explanation: STEWARDESSES (12 letters) is the longest common English word typed with only the left hand! S-T-E-W-A-R-D-E-S-S-E-S — all left-hand keys on QWERTY. Perfect for those with a Castle in their right hand!

questions.question: English has 26 letters. How many of them are NOT used as a Roman numeral (I, V, X, L, C, D, M)?
questions.explanation: Roman numerals use: I, V, X, L, C, D, M = 7 letters. So 26 - 7 = 19 letters are NOT Roman numerals. Simple subtraction, but you need to know your Roman numeral set!
